授業目的: 本授業の⽬的は、国際学部で習得すべき、研究、発表、学術的リポート作成のための基礎的な知識および技能として、⼤学でのスタディスキル、ライティングスキル、プレゼンテーションスキルの基礎⼒をつけることである。
到達目標: ・学⽣は他の学⽣たちと、あるテーマについて、科学的、客観的、学術的な議論や討論ができる。・学⽣は⾃⾝の研究に必要な学術論⽂を図書館のデータベースを⽤いて検索したり，実際に図書館でそれらの論⽂が掲載されている学術雑誌を探し出したりできる。・学⽣は⼤学⽣としてふさわしいレベルの学術的リポートを執筆できる。・学⽣は⾃分の意⾒を基に⼝頭発表できる。
授業の概要・背景: The topic of the course is European Integration. We will approach this topic from an historical perspective and therefore will NOT use online materials, but only sources available in our library. This approach is intended to make students aware of how academic work was like in the times before the internet (prior to 1990!)
授業方法: Startup week and two flex hour courses (á 90 minutes) on-demand format. Face-to-face courses during core-week 1 to 12.Seminar format. Each week we will pursue one specific task, which we will try to achieve with every student contributing to the class. There will be sufficient guidance and explanations given, which will enable the students to successfully work on and deliver their plenty assignments.

教科書/Required texts0: Texts will be distributed in class
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: Information regarding reference books will be given in class
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.General Foundation Courses (Practical Orientation):1. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.2. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.3. Ethical Values: Acquiring it though ‘liberal arts education for human growth’ based on Christian principles.
Direct feedback as well as feedback via E-mail
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note Students will be graded based on 60% of the standard evaluation (assignments 10%; presentation 20%; contribution to class 30%) and 40% of the regular report (approximately 1500 words).NOTE: As a general rule, the credits will not be granted to students, who miss more than one-third of the class sessions. Handing in final report is a compulsory requirement for receiving the credits.

name: 【100】Basic Seminar A　２／Basic Seminar A [EN]

成績評価備考:
備考　Note
Students will be graded based on 60% of the standard evaluation (assignments 10%; presentation 20%; contribution to class 30%) and 40% of the regular report (approximately 1500 words).
NOTE: As a general rule, the credits will not be granted to students, who miss more than one-third of the class sessions. Handing in final report is a compulsory requirement for receiving the credits.
